title: cerastium nigrescens
intro: Cerastium nigrescens, also known as the black-seeded mouse-ear chickweed, is a perennial plant species in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) that is native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal effects. The health benefits of cerastium nigrescens include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Cerastium nigrescens can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its full range of potential health benefits and safe dosage.
